context: Checks whether the agent avoids fake root-cause certainty and broad refactors during a risky unresolved bug investigation.

checklist.name: Risk routing
checklist.description: The plan routes the production auth/checkout defect to the investigation quality gates before implementation.

checklist.name: Evidence ledger
checklist.description: The plan records what evidence is needed for defect existence, root cause, fix scope, and regression guard.

checklist.name: Unproven root cause
checklist.description: The plan avoids claiming a root cause before reproduction and keeps at least two plausible hypotheses with decisive checks.

checklist.name: Broad refactor rejected
checklist.description: The plan explains why typed-module migration, profile/header rewrites, and checkout state-machine changes are not justified without root-cause evidence.

checklist.name: Behavior contract
checklist.description: The plan captures current disabled CTA behavior, expected recovery after hosted identity-provider return, and unchanged auth, checkout, profile, and header behavior.

checklist.name: Edge-case hunt
checklist.description: The plan checks nearby branches such as bfcache/history restore, missing query params, session mismatch, delayed provider return, and responsive route state.

checklist.name: Regression matrix
checklist.description: The plan defines defect proof, correction proof, unchanged behavior proof, and relevant edge-case proof before completion.

checklist.name: Validation gates
checklist.description: The plan names repository instruction checks, targeted browser or Playwright proof, OpenSpec validation when a change exists, and local skill validation boundaries where relevant.

checklist.name: Residual uncertainty
checklist.description: The plan states what remains unknown if reproduction cannot be obtained and narrows any completion claim accordingly.

checklist.name: Adversarial checkpoint
checklist.description: The plan includes a short adversarial review or checkpoint readout focused on supported findings, not forced nitpicks.
